pages.raw_text:
LESS THAN 80 per cent of the
voters of the city yesterday de-
creed that the new city charter
should be adopted, and it is now
the fundamental law of the city


In registration of about 10000,
a little over half. or 10,463 votes
were cast, of which $3.50 were for
the new charter and Alb against
it.


All of the organization, effort
and campaigning were on the side
of the charter. The opposition was
and made practically
no fight at the polls. The talk of

ment organizing to beat the char
ter was bosh. They did nothing,
